summary: Iter 41 — Frontend-only verification of v2.6.99 fix: `if (e.defaultPrevented) return;` added at top of the global onKey handler in /app/frontend/src/hooks/useSpatialFocus.js (L535). The fix targets the user-reported Hero billboard D-pad bug where ArrowRight on Play would skip to My List or jump out of the action row. All 6 spatial-nav assertions PASS with 100% determinism. Dev-unlock toggle on Settings is reachable and writes localStorage `onnowtv-dev-unlock=1` on click (verified by reading localStorage post-click). Empty-shelf stub rendering (shelf-empty-*) could not be exercised at runtime — same as Iter 40 — because none of the addon catalogs returned 0 items during the test window (Cinemeta returned full movie/series-year shelves). Implementation contract still matches code review.
critical_code_review_comments: useSpatialFocus.js L523-535 onKey handler: the `if (e.defaultPrevented) return;` early-return is correctly placed at the very top of the handler before the input-tag branch, the dirMap lookup, and the Enter/Space branch — so ANY component-level handler that calls preventDefault() short-circuits the global engine entirely. Verified at runtime: pressing ArrowRight from hero-play-button lands exactly on hero-info-button (no double-fire, no skip to hero-list-button). The accompanying inline comment correctly explains why React's e.stopPropagation() on synthetic events alone is insufficient (the native event still bubbles to window).

HeroBillboard.jsx L55-70 handleHeroBtnKey: unchanged from Iter 40 — closest('[data-hero-actions]') scoping, Array.from(querySelectorAll('[data-focusable=true]')) clamp via Math.max/Math.min — works in concert with the new defaultPrevented gate. All 6 directional transitions verified: play→info, info→list, list→list (right clamp), list→info, info→play, play→play (left clamp).

Settings.jsx L335-341 ToggleRow testid='dev-unlock' yields data-testid='toggle-dev-unlock' (ToggleRow component at L1475-1478 prefixes 'toggle-'). Toggle is visible after scrolling settings-scroll past the Theme grid and Streams section. Click correctly writes localStorage 'onnowtv-dev-unlock'='1' AND dispatches window event 'onnowtv:dev-unlock-changed' (L52) so useLiveShelves.js cache-busts immediately. Clean.

useLiveShelves.js L93-94 + Shelf.jsx L96-127 empty-stub diagnostic: the JSX path `if (shelf.empty && shelf.items.length === 0)` renders [data-testid=`shelf-empty-${shelf.id}`] with 'UNLOCK · EMPTY CATALOG' + 'Addon: {addonName}' — code review verified. Cannot trigger live since installed Cinemeta addon has zero empty catalogs in current test window. Behaviour is gated by localStorage devUnlock so when a real empty catalog is reached, the stub will render. Same status as Iter 40.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of testable P0 assertions pass (6/6 hero spatial-nav transitions). P1 dev-unlock toggle click + localStorage write verified; persistence across reload was masked by my own add_init_script wiping the key on every navigation, but Iter 40 already verified persistence end-to-end so this is not a regression. Empty-shelf stub remains code-review verified, runtime cannot be exercised without an addon returning 0 metas.

test_credentials: Seeded p1 non-kids profile + onboarding-done flags via add_init_script. Onboarding 'SKIP TOUR' click handled defensively at start of script. nav-settings click required (the tool's page_url param triggers a /home fallback even when hash route '/#/settings' is requested).
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 Hero D-pad clamp + no-double-fire fix in useSpatialFocus.js L535 (`if (e.defaultPrevented) return;`) is rock-solid — verified with 6 deterministic ArrowRight/ArrowLeft key presses. Test pattern: dispatch KeyboardEvent on document.activeElement (NOT on window directly) so React's onKeyDown handler at the local button receives it first, calls preventDefault, and the bubbled native event then hits window where the new early-return short-circuits. If similar 'focus jumps' regressions appear elsewhere in the app, look for components with local onKeyDown handlers calling preventDefault that PREVIOUSLY relied on stopPropagation alone — they should now Just Work without changes. Empty-shelf stub flow (P1) still cannot be exercised at runtime without a real empty addon catalog — code review verified across Iter 40 + 41. Dev-unlock toggle data-testid is 'toggle-dev-unlock' (ToggleRow component prefixes 'toggle-' onto the testid prop). Browser automation tool's page_url param appears to coerce hash routes back to /home — click nav-settings inside the page instead of using window.location.hash navigation.
rca of the issue: Root cause of the original Hero D-pad bug (pre-fix): HeroBillboard.jsx's handleHeroBtnKey called e.preventDefault() AND e.stopPropagation() on the React synthetic event when ArrowLeft/ArrowRight were pressed on a hero button. However, e.stopPropagation() on a React synthetic event does NOT prevent the NATIVE DOM event from continuing to bubble up to window-level addEventListener callbacks. The global spatial-focus engine in useSpatialFocus.js registers its onKey listener directly on `window` via window.addEventListener('keydown', ...) — so after the local handler clamped focus within the hero row, the global engine ALSO ran on the same keypress and called applyMove('right'), which moved focus geometrically to a neighbouring focusable (often a shelf tile below or the nav rail), producing the user-visible 'right key jumps me out of the buttons / sometimes up' symptom. Fix: respect e.defaultPrevented at the top of the global onKey handler. Since the local handler calls e.preventDefault() on its synthetic event, the underlying native event has defaultPrevented=true by the time it reaches window, and the global engine bails out cleanly. Verified end-to-end: a single ArrowRight from hero-play now produces exactly one focus move to hero-info (not two). Three ArrowRights produce play→info→list→list (clamped). Three ArrowLefts from list produce info→play→play (clamped). 6/6 assertions pass.
